name: Coagulation factor V activity
description: Functional clotting activity of factor V ; distinct from factor V antigen (immunological quantity)

concept.conceptName: Coagulation factor V activity actual/normal in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay
Coagulation factor V activated [Units/volume] in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay

en.name: Coagulation factor V activity
en.shortDescription: Functional clotting activity of factor V ; distinct from factor V antigen (immunological quantity)
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Coagulation factor V is a clotting protein synthesised mainly by the liver [1]. Its **activity** — what this concept set measures — reflects the functional capacity of the protein in the clotting cascade [1]. The result is a single numeric value per patient-timestamp, produced by a coagulation analyser on platelet-poor plasma using a one-stage clot-based assay [1, 2]. Results are reported either as a percentage of normal plasma activity or in IU/mL [3]. **Note: activity vs antigen.** This concept set covers **functional activity** only — what the factor V protein actually does in the clotting cascade. The antigenic concentration of factor V (immunological measurement of protein mass irrespective of activity) is captured by a separate concept set, "Coagulation factor V antigen". The two values can diverge in qualitative deficits where antigen is normal but activity is reduced — they are different study variables and must not be merged. ## Included Concepts The set contains two LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, both measuring functional factor V activity in platelet-poor plasma by clot-based coagulation assay, differing only by the result representation: - *3005757 / 3193-0 — Coagulation factor V activity actual/normal in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay (LOINC)*: result expressed as a **percentage** of pooled normal plasma activity (e.g. 75 %). Most widely used representation, returned by routine coagulation analysers. - *3006924 / 3192-2 — Coagulation factor V activated [Units/volume] in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay (LOINC)*: result expressed in **U/mL** or **IU/mL** (e.g. 0.75 IU/mL). Less common representation; numerically equivalent to the percentage divided by 100. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC: **`%`** (concept 8554) for `3193-0`. For `3192-2`, the recommended unit is `[arb'U]/mL` (arbitrary unit per millilitre, equivalent to IU/mL). Source systems may report `U/mL`, `IU/mL` or `[IU]/mL` interchangeably. ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The following LOINC concepts that exist for factor V are deliberately not part of this set: - **Factor V antigen** (`3027396 / 3194-8 — Coagulation factor V Ag [Units/volume]`, `3023945 / 3195-5 — Coagulation factor V Ag actual/normal`): captured by the separate concept set "Coagulation factor V antigen". - **Factor V activated** (`3006924 / 3192-2`): NB — included above. The activated form measured here is the assay readout, not factor Va in vivo. - **Factor V inhibitor** (`3004057 / 3191-4 — Coagulation factor V inhibitor [Units/volume]`, `21494686 / 81124-0 — Coagulation factor V inhibitor [Presence]`): out of scope; describes acquired antibodies against factor V (rare bleeding disorder), not the factor V activity itself. - **Factor V activity and inhibitor panel** (`36031832 / 96458-5`): a panel grouping concept, not a measurement result. - **Factor V Leiden mutation** (a separate genetic test): not a coagulation measurement; out of scope. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "factor V", "FV", "facteur V" or "factor V activity" without further specification, with the value expressed as a percentage of normal: `3005757 / 3193-0 — Coagulation factor V activity actual/normal in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay`. By far the most common LOINC code. - **Source reports IU/mL or U/mL**: map to `3006924 / 3192-2 — Coagulation factor V activated [Units/volume] in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay`. If the source provides both the % and the IU/mL representation as separate columns, store both — they are equivalent but allow consumers to choose. - **Do not confuse with**: factor V antigen (different concept set), factor V Leiden genotype (genetic test, not coagulation), factor V inhibitor (autoantibody titer), factor V deficient plasma reagent (laboratory reagent, not a patient measurement). - **Method preservation**: when the source records the assay method (one-stage clot vs chromogenic, deficient plasma reagent supplier, etc.), preserve it in the measurement source value — it does not change the target concept. ## References [1] Mann KG, Kalafatis M. Factor V: a combination of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde. *Blood*. 2003;101(1):20-30. Available: https://doi.org/10.1182/blood-2002-01-0290 [2] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 3193-0 — Coagulation factor V activity actual/normal in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay (Part description for "Coagulation factor V activity"). Available: https://loinc.org/3193-0/ [3] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
